title: Reading B1 - The London Eye
description: Diagnostic reading test for B1 level
passage: The London Eye, also known as the Millennium Wheel, is a giant observation wheel located on the South Bank of the River Thames in London. It was opened to the public on March 9, 2000, and has become one of the most popular tourist attractions in the city. The wheel is 135 metres tall and has 32 passenger capsules. Each capsule can hold up to 25 people. A complete rotation takes approximately 30 minutes, giving visitors enough time to enjoy views of famous landmarks such as Big Ben, St Paul's Cathedral, and Buckingham Palace.

questions.question: Where is the London Eye located?
options: On the South Bank of the River Thames

questions.question: When was the London Eye opened to the public?
How tall is the London Eye?
How many passenger capsules does it have?
How long does a complete rotation take?
